---
title: "macOS Setup Guide"
description: "Install Git, configure your terminal, and set up SSH authentication on macOS."
platform: macos
---

## What You Will Install

| Tool | Version | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| Xcode Command Line Tools | Latest | Provides Git and core Unix tools |
| Homebrew | Latest | Package manager (optional but recommended) |
| Git | ≥ 2.28 | Version control |

## Step 1: Open Terminal

Press **Command + Space**, type `Terminal`, and press **Return**.

You should see a window with a prompt ending in `$` or `%` (zsh default on macOS 10.15+).

## Step 2: Install Git

macOS ships with an older Git via Xcode Command Line Tools. Install the latest version via Homebrew for best compatibility.

**Option A — Homebrew (recommended)**:

```bash
# Install Homebrew if you don't have it
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install Git
brew install git
```

**Option B — Xcode Command Line Tools only**:

```bash
xcode-select --install
```

A dialog will appear — click "Install".

## Step 3: Verify Installation

```bash
git --version
```

```text
git version 2.44.0
```

Your version number may differ. Anything ≥ 2.28 is supported.

```bash
which git
```

```text
/opt/homebrew/bin/git
```

If you used Option B, you will see `/usr/bin/git` — that is also fine.

## Step 4: Configure Git

Tell Git who you are. These values appear in every commit you make.

```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your@institution.edu"
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global core.editor nano
```

Verify your configuration:

```bash
git config --list
```

```text
user.name=Your Name
user.email=your@institution.edu
init.defaultBranch=main
core.editor=nano
```

## Step 5: Generate an SSH Key

SSH keys let you authenticate with GitHub without entering a password every time.

```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your@institution.edu"
```

When prompted:

- **File location**: Press **Return** to accept the default (`~/.ssh/id_ed25519`)
- **Passphrase**: Enter a passphrase (recommended) or press **Return** twice to skip

```text
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Enter file in which to save the key (/Users/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519):
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase):
Enter same passphrase again:
Your identification has been saved in /Users/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519
Your public key has been saved in /Users/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

## Step 6: Add SSH Key to GitHub

Copy your public key to the clipboard:

```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

```text
ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3Nza... your@institution.edu
```

Select and copy the entire output. Then:

1. Go to **github.com → Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key**
2. Title: `My MacBook` (or whatever describes your machine)
3. Paste the key
4. Click **Add SSH key**

## Step 7: Verify SSH Connection

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

```text
Hi username! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.
```

If you see this message, SSH is working correctly.

## Troubleshooting

**`git: command not found`**
: Git is not on your PATH. If you used Homebrew, run `brew doctor` to check your PATH configuration. Restart your terminal after installing.

**`Permission denied (publickey)`**
: Your SSH key is not registered with GitHub, or the key agent is not running. Run `ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519` to add your key to the agent, then try again.

**`brew: command not found`**
: Homebrew did not install or is not on PATH. Follow the Homebrew installation instructions at [brew.sh](https://brew.sh) and restart your terminal.
